issue_title: Manage folders does not switch accounts

reasoning_trace.finding: Confirmed the repo is Thunderbird for Android/K-9 Mail shared codebase; the app supports multiple accounts and legacy K-9 code remains part of the app.
Confirmed contribution context and that implementation may span current feature modules and legacy modules.
Confirmed newer feature/core API-internal boundaries; legacy modules are still out of scope for this split, so a folder-management UI under legacy is plausible.
Search found folder-management strings and a legacy UI class named FolderSettingsFragment; this indicated the Manage folders screen likely lives under legacy/ui/legacy.

Identified relevant files: legacy/ui/legacy/src/main/java/com/fsck/k9/ui/managefolders/ManageFoldersActivity.kt, ManageFoldersFragment.kt, ManageFoldersViewModel.kt, FolderSettingsFragment.kt, FolderSettingsViewModel.kt, FolderSettingsDataStore.kt, KoinModule.kt, and FolderListItem.kt.

FolderSettingsFragment reads EXTRA_ACCOUNT and EXTRA_FOLDER_ID from navigation arguments, then calls viewModel.getFolderSettingsLiveData(accountUuid, folderId). It does not independently choose the first account.

FolderSettingsViewModel loads the account by the provided UUID via preferences.getAccount(accountUuid), then loads folder details for account.id. This code uses the supplied account argument correctly.
FolderSettingsDataStore updates folder settings using the accountId passed from FolderSettingsViewModel. No account switching logic exists here.
ManageFoldersActivity reads EXTRA_ACCOUNT from its Intent and passes it into the navigation graph as ManageFoldersFragment.EXTRA_ACCOUNT. Its launch() helper writes account.uuid into the Intent.

ManageFoldersFragment loads account = preferences.getAccount(accountUuid) in onCreate(), displays folders via viewModel.getFolders(account), and passes account.uuid to FolderSettingsFragment. The fragment uses the Intent-provided account consistently.
ManageFoldersViewModel delegates to folderRepository.getDisplayFoldersFlow(account, includeHiddenFolders = true), so wrong folders result from receiving the wrong account object.
The manage-folders view models are normal Koin view models with injected repositories/controllers; no suspicious singleton account state is injected.
Found the only launch path in legacy/ui/legacy/src/main/java/com/fsck/k9/activity/MessageHomeActivity.kt and the drawer event/effect path in feature/navigation/drawer/dropdown.
Drawer state contains selectedAccountId, but Effect.OpenManageFolders is a data object carrying no account id.
DrawerViewModel handles Event.SelectAccount by updating state.value.selectedAccountId. However Event.OnManageFoldersClick emits Effect.OpenManageFolders without including selectedAccountId.
DrawerView maps Effect.OpenManageFolders to openManageFolders(), a zero-argument callback, so the drawer-selected account cannot reach the activity.

DropDownDrawer stores FolderDrawerState(selectedAccountUuid, selectedFolderId), exposes selectAccount/selectFolder/selectUnifiedInbox, but its openManageFolders callback also has no account parameter.
MessageHomeActivity owns a separate private var account: LegacyAccountDto? initialized from launch/search state. This is not the same as the drawer view model's selectedAccountId.
The drawer is constructed with openManageFolders = { launchManageFoldersScreen() }, so clicking Manage folders calls the activity method without the drawer-selected account id.
The method checks account != null and then calls ManageFoldersActivity.launch(this, account!!), using the activity's current/stale account field rather than the drawer-selected account.
Opening a real account can update search/activity state via performSearch(), but merely changing the drawer-selected account does not necessarily update MessageHomeActivity.account.

configureDrawer() pushes the activity account/search into the drawer via drawer.selectAccount(accountUuid). This proves the activity account drives drawer initial state, while drawer-only selection is separate state.
Although this helper writes account.uuid into the Intent, it is correct for the account it receives. The faulty value is already chosen by the caller.
The fragment loads folders for the UUID argument it receives. It does not default to the first account unless the caller passed that first/default account UUID.

This directly matches the report: after app start the activity account is the first/default account; selecting another account in the drawer changes drawer state, but Manage folders still launches with the stale activity account.

fault_candidates.reasoning: This statement launches ManageFoldersActivity with MessageHomeActivity.account, which is derived from the current search/default launch state and can remain the first account from app startup. The drawer-selected account is held separately in DrawerViewModel.selectedAccountId and is not passed through the OpenManageFolders effect, so this statement supplies the wrong account UUID to the Manage folders screen.

This emits a manage-folders effect without the selectedAccountId even though the view model knows it. It is a contributing design flaw, but the concrete wrong account is finally passed at MessageHomeActivity.launchManageFoldersScreen().

final_answer.causal_chain: The user changes the selected account in the navigation drawer. DrawerViewModel records that account in selectedAccountId, but OnManageFoldersClick emits OpenManageFolders without an account id. DrawerView maps that to a zero-argument openManageFolders callback. MessageHomeActivity receives that callback and launchManageFoldersScreen() uses its own account field, which was initialized from app startup/search state and can still be the first/default account. ManageFoldersActivity receives that stale account UUID, ManageFoldersFragment loads that account, and ManageFoldersViewModel displays that account's folders.
final_answer.fix_hint: Pass the drawer-selected account id through the OpenManageFolders effect/callback and launch ManageFoldersActivity with that account instead of MessageHomeActivity.account.
